rationale: Ground truth closes this as a duplicate of #4808 with details in #4809; Y's answer converges on exactly that outcome and correctly reconstructs the #4809/#4810/#4808 relationship (4 of 5 loaders fixed in #4810, this DiT site scoped to #4808), while honestly flagging what it could and couldn't verify (rebase branch vs. upstream main) and surfacing a real test-coverage gap. X instead asserts the bug is 'already fixed on main' with specific line numbers, code snippets, and a 'zero grep hits' claim presented as verified fact, but never actually reconciles this with the real resolution (duplicate/#4808) and its confident specifics read as fabricated rather than grounded. Y is more aligned with the actual thread, more transparent about evidence limits, and adds legitimate follow-up value; X's confident but likely-invented verification detail is the weaker, riskier answer.
